item.title: 150 army officers ousted during AL era get retirement benefits
item.description: The government granted retrospective promotions, arrears and normal retirement status to 150 former officers of the Bangladesh Army, Navy and Air Force who had been forced to retire, removed, discharged or dismissed from service during the Awami League rule....

item.title: Fresh push revives hopes of easing gridlocks
item.description: The government’s renewed push to relocate inter-district bus terminals outside Dhaka has revived hopes of easing the capital city’s perennial gridlock after over a decade of delays caused by bureaucratic hurdles, poor planning, land acquisition problems and institutional inertia....

item.title: Tourists flock to Starbucks overlooking North Korea
item.description: The contrast cannot be starker: selfie-taking tourists sipping coffee at Starbucks — an icon of globalisation and capitalism — while looking out over reclusive, communist North Korea....

item.title: Mythos and the fight over America's most powerful AI
item.description: After a US government greenlight, global access to Anthropic's powerful AI model Fable 5 — along with restricted access to the even more powerful Mythos 5 — has been restored, following a standoff that strained ties between Washington and Silicon Valley....

item.title: Fear and anger brew inside Meta amid AI frenzy
item.description: A frenzied push for artificial intelligence dominance comes with a different kind of cost for Meta, where massive layoffs, employee surveillance and departures have fuelled reports of a heated internal climate....

item.title: The Afghan women farmers keeping their village alive
item.description: In a remote province of northeastern Afghanistan, women farmers are playing a vital role in their community's survival among the snow-capped mountains....

item.title: Cuba gets private sector makeover
item.description: From grocery stores to restaurants and fuel, Cuba's burgeoning private sector is filling the vacuum left by a collapsing communist-run state....

item.title: Russia's small businesses pay price of spiralling Ukraine war
item.description: In a Moscow suburb, shut-down shops and ‘for rent’ signs reveal the creeping toll of Russia's slowing wartime economy....

item.title: Strategic chokepoint Hormuz in shadow of geopolitics
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for the global trade of crude oil and liquefied natural gas, is at the centre of disputes in negotiations to end the Middle East war, even after the US and Iran signed a preliminary deal....

item.title: Chinese investors flock to Hong Kong as trading curbs tighten
item.description: Soon after disembarking at Hong Kong’s train station, Chinese private investor Feng was opening a stock trading account at a nearby brokerage, hoping to evade tighter restrictions on capital leaving the country....

item.title: Global stocks mostly rise as beaten-down tech stocks enjoy bounce
item.description: Major stock markets mostly rose Friday as tech firms rebounded, while a sizeable miss on US jobs creation soothed worries over a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike....

item.title: Creaky private credit sector continues to worry investors
item.description: An admired symbol of financial innovation in recent years, private credit has become a source of unease in US financial markets, and a subject of intensifying scrutiny....

item.title: MFS provider bkash honours 100 distribution house employees
item.description: Mobile financial service provider bkash recently honoured 100 employees working under bKash distribution houses for their creative...

item.title: SJIB Foundation gives Tk 11 lakh to madrasah in Narsingdi
item.description: Shahjalal Islami Bank Foundation provided a financial contribution of Tk 11 lakh to meet the development work and operational expenses of Misbahul Ulum Model Madrasah in Panchdona Union under Madhabdi of Narsingdi district....

item.title: AIB holds 461st board meeting
item.description: Al-Arafah Islami Bank PLC on Monday held its 461st board meeting at its head office in the capital, said a press release....

item.title: India takes down battery apps after stalled e-rickshaw rides
item.description: Indian officials said Friday they had taken down two smartphone apps following reports that some e-rickshaws could be remotely shut down, exploiting a security gap in the budget vehicles....

item.title: Rangpur farmers exceed jute cultivation target
item.description: For the first time in recent years, farmers have exceeded the jute cultivation target in the Rangpur agricultural region this season....

item.title: Carney secures deal for western Canada oil pipeline
item.description: Canadian prime minister Mark Carney secured an investment agreement with British Columbia on Thursday to build a major oil pipeline, overcoming initial opposition from the westernmost province....

item.title: BRAC Bank unveils offers for 25th anniv
item.description: BRAC Bank has unveiled a range of offers for its customers to mark its 25th anniversary, celebrating the trust and loyalty of the millions of customers who have been part of the bank’s journey over the years....

item.title: Islami Bank, BPDB sign corporate deal
item.description: Islami Bank Bangladesh PLC and Bangladesh Power Development Board signed a corporate agreement regarding customer service on Thursday at Bidyut Bhaban in the capital, said a press release....GeographicReference

item.title: When children are the target
item.description: THE United Nations Independent International Commission of Inquiry on the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including East Jerusalem, and Israel has released one of the most devastating reports ever produced by a UN investigative body on the Israeli genocide in Gaza....

item.title: Not geopolitical realignment
item.description: SPEARHEADED by Beijing, the proposed China-Myanmar-Bangladesh Economic Corridor is a multilateral infrastructure and trade initiative designed to link China’s landlocked south-western Yunnan province directly to the Bay of Bengal. Formally introduced during......

item.title: CHINA’S STRATEGY: Oil, risk and transition
item.description: CHINA is the world’s largest importer of crude oil, at about 11.8 million barrels per day, while its consumption stands at roughly 14.8 million barrels a day, according to the Energy Institute’s Statistical Review of World Energy 2025. However, in recent years, China has......

item.title: OFFSHORE GAS: Theft abroad or failure at home?
item.description: A CLAIM is moving from social media chatter into mainstream discussion: Bangladesh’s gas, it is said, is being stolen from beneath the Bay of Bengal. The fear is easy to understand. People watch India and Myanmar produce offshore oil and gas on either side of our waters......

item.title: Govt should change obsolete railway law to better services
item.description: THE state of the Bangladesh Railway’s legal framework is concerning. ANew Agereport says that efforts by successive governments to amends the 136-year-old British-era railway law with a view to improving passenger services, safety and preventing fraud have remained......GeographicReference

item.title: Economy cannot afford easing cost-cutting measures
item.description: THE government’s apparent decision to discontinue austerity measures raises concern about its commitment to prudent public finance at a time when restraint remain an economic necessity amid persistent fiscal pressure, uncertain global energy markets and......

item.title: Salah says 'had to do it' after coolest of penalties
item.description:
A nerveless Mohamed Salah said he decided only at the last moment to chip in his penalty under massive pressure to help Egypt into the World Cup last 16 on Friday.
...

item.title: France braced for extreme heat threat against Paraguay
item.description: France coach Didier Deschamps promised Friday his team would be able to adapt to the potential impact of extreme weather on their World Cup last-16 tie against Paraguay on Saturday as a brutal heatwave hits the northeastern United States....

item.title: Egypt edge Australia on penalties to reach last 16
item.description:
Hossam Abdelmaguid scored the winning penalty as Egypt made history by beating a dogged Australia 4-2 on penalties to reach the World Cup last 16 on Friday.
...

item.title: England's Kane feels 'as good as ever' ahead of Mexico clash
item.description:
Harry Kane says he feels ‘as good as I've ever felt’ as he hunts more World Cup goals for England to extend his remarkable season.
...

item.title: England v Mexico World Cup kickoff could be moved earlier: source
item.description:
England's World Cup last-16 match against Mexico on Sunday could be moved to an earlier kickoff time due to the risk of storms and flooding, a source close to the arrangements said.
...

item.title: BUP holds 18th annual senate meeting
item.description: The Bangladesh University of Professionals has organised the 18th annual senate meeting at the Senate Hall of BUP in Dhaka recently....

item.title: UGC plans 14-storey building to solve staff housing crisis
item.description: The University Grants Commission of Bangladesh has initiated a plan to construct a 14-story residential building under its own funding to resolve a long-standing housing crisis for its staff....

item.title: Protesters block Sylhet highway over truck seizures
item.description: Local workers kept the Sylhet-Tamabil highway blocked for around two hours on Friday morning by unloading stones from a truck on the highway, protesting the seizure of vehicles loaded with illegally extracted stones by Border Guard Bangladesh at Jaintapur upazila in Sylhet....

item.title: NDC delegation visits BRRI, BARI
item.description: A 145-member delegation from the National Defence Course 2026, comprising senior military and civil officials from 18 countries, visited the Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute and the Bangladesh Rice Research Institute in Gazipur on Wednesday....

item.title: 6 more held over Adabor BNP leader murder
item.description: The law enforcers have detained six more suspects in connection with the murder of a local leader of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, allegedly stemming from a dispute over a recent FIFA World Cup match in the capital’s Adabor....

item.title: Ctg Port shifts to automated operations
item.description: The Chittagong Port Authority has transitioned to a fully automated, paperless operation by upgrading its terminal operating system to eliminate all manual and paper-based delivery documentation....

item.title: Strong media indispensable for vibrant democracy: Fakhrul
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Friday called on journalists to uphold objectivity and impartiality in reporting, saying a strong and independent media is essential for a vibrant democracy....

item.title: Elite Group to establish endowment fund for DU students
item.description: Elite Group will set up a Tk 2 crore endowment fund to support the underprivileged students at the University of Dhaka....

item.title: BARI launches tree plantation programme
item.description: The Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute inaugurated a tree plantation programme at its head office in Gazipur on Thursday....

item.title: Abdul Hakim Bikrampuri’s death anniv today
item.description: Today is the 39th anniversary of the death of Abdul Hakim Bikrampuri, member of the then Bengal Legislative Assembly, East Bengal Provincial Assembly and the 3rd National Assembly of Pakistan....

item.title: CU holds free medical camp
item.description: The Female Students’ Welfare department of the Chittagong University Central Students’ Union held a day-long free medical camp for female students at the university in Chattogram on Thursday....

item.title: Aziz Al Kaiser made NSU BoT chairman
item.description: The board of trustees of North South University has nominated Aziz Al Kaiser to continue as chairman of the NSU trust for the 2026–2027 tenure....

item.title: Kamruddin Absar worked to shape mass culture: discussion
item.description: People’s song singer Kamruddin Absar used his political ideology to shape the culture of the masses in the country....

item.title: 5 killed in road accidents in 3 districts
item.description: At least five people, including two children, were killed and several others injured in road accidents in Madaripur, Gazipur and Noakhali on Friday....

item.title: Rajshahi research centre revolutionises fruit farming
item.description: The Regional Fruit Research Centre in Binodpur of Rajshahi is spearheading a quiet revolution in fruit cultivation through the use of modern technology....

item.title: Bangladesh likely to face flooding in July-August
item.description: Bangladesh may witness flooding in the Brahmaputra and Meghna River basins in July and August this year due to heavy precipitation in their upper catchment areas, according to the Flood Forecasting and Warning Centre....

item.title: Amended laws fail to curb noise pollution in Dhaka
item.description: Ramjan Ali from Muktagachha in Mymensingh, attending to his tuberculosis-affected wife at Dhaka Medical College Hospital for the past seven days, described the noise situation on Thursday as ‘intolerable’....

item.title: Myanmar president attends first ASEAN summit
item.description: Myanmar coup chief turned president Min Aung Hlaing landed in Laos on Friday, officials said, in his first state visit to an ASEAN country since claiming the post of civilian leader....

item.title: NOVOAir flight makes emergency landing in Dhaka
item.description: A NOVOAir flight bound for Cox’s Bazar made an emergency landing at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka Friday evening after developing a technical fault shortly after takeoff, with prime minister’s adviser on foreign affairs and civil aviation Humayun Kabir among the passengers....

item.title: UN warns of extreme weather in Jul-Sept
item.description: El Nino is here and will quickly develop into a strong event between July and September, fuelling the likelihood of extreme weather, the United Nations’ climate agency warned Friday....

item.title: Army chief returns home after Czech, Slovakia visit
item.description: Chief of the Bangladesh Army General Waker-Uz-Zaman returned home on Friday, following an official visit to the Czech Republic and Slovakia....

item.title: Brazil, Argentina envoys highlight ties thru football
item.description: The ambassadors of Brazil and Argentina on Friday put emphasis on football’s role in promoting peace, unity and friendship among nations as Bangladesh’s first International Airlines Football Championship 2026 kicked off in Dhaka....

item.title: Media objectivity key to governance: info minister
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Friday stressed that responsible, accurate and objective journalism is essential for helping citizens understand state governance and make informed decisions, warning that distorted information can create confusion, conflict and social disorder....

item.title: Special mangrove plantation drive launched at Mongla
item.description: A special mangrove plantation programme has been launched in Mongla of Bagerhat to strengthen the climate resilience of coastal communities and help reduce the impacts of natural disasters....

item.title: 10 killed as Russia, Ukraine trade strikes
item.description: At least 10 people were killed in Ukrainian attacks on Russia and Moscow-occupied territory, local officials said Friday, as Ukraine reported five killed in Russian attacks....

item.title: PM to join remembrance programme for July uprising martyrs today
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman will join a special commemoration programme in Dhaka today to honour the martyrs of the July Mass Uprising....

item.title: KHAMENEI FUNERAL: World leaders gather in Iran
item.description: Top Iranian officials on Friday paid their final respects to the country’s slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei at the start of a week of funeral ceremonies, with allies and mourners converging on Tehran where his body is lying in state....
